title: Gramática · English C1
scope: C1 (Cambridge Advanced) añade inversión, oraciones hendidas, condicionales avanzados y mixtos con conjunciones alternativas, oraciones de participio, estructuras enfáticas, modalidad avanzada, elipsis y sustitución, nominalización y marcadores del discurso. Límite: produces textos fluidos, bien organizados y sensibles al registro; C2 refina el idioma y la sutileza estilística.

blocks.text: Tras adverbios negativos o limitativos al inicio de la oración, el sujeto y el auxiliar se invierten. Es formal y enfático.

rows: Never have I seen such a thing.
Hardly had I arrived when it started.
Not only did he apologise, but he also paid.
Only after the meeting did I understand.

blocks.text: Divide una oración para enfatizar una parte, usando It is/was … that … o What … is ….
It was John who broke the window. What I need is a holiday.
Enlaces condicionales alternativos y estructuras más formales.

rows: I won't go unless you come.
You can stay as long as you are quiet.
If she were to call, tell her I'm out.
Had I known, I would have helped.

blocks.text: Usa un participio presente o pasado para acortar una oración, habitual en la escritura.
Feeling tired, she went to bed. (= Because she felt tired …)
Built in 1850, the bridge is still in use. (= The bridge, which was built in 1850, …)
El auxiliar do/does/did y el fronting añaden énfasis sin cambiar el significado básico.
I do appreciate your help. Never did I imagine this. That, I cannot accept.
Omite palabras repetidas (elipsis) o sustitúyelas con so/one/do para evitar repetición.
She can sing and so can I. "Is it raining?" — "I think so." I'd like the red one.
Conectores formales estructuran argumentos y contrastes en la escritura.

rows: It was hard; nonetheless, we finished.
He is calm, whereas she is nervous.
She invested early, thereby securing profit.

blocks.text: Convertir verbos/adjetivos en sustantivos para crear un estilo académico más denso y formal.
Prices increased rapidly → The rapid increase in prices … · They decided to … → Their decision to …
